judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content: a black-and-white sketch of a large, faceted dome or sphere structure suspended above a horizon by a central tower-like object and a base, but they differ on specific details like the presence of exhaust plumes and the title text.
original_image_tags: A black-and-white technical illustration or diagram, oriented landscape. The diagram is rendered in white lines and shading on a black background. At the top center, a cylindrical object resembling a rocket or missile is shown emitting exhaust or a plume upwards. Dashed lines radiate from the top and bottom of this object, extending outwards to the edges of a large, dome-like structure below. This dome appears to be a wireframe or geodesic sphere, showing its internal structural lines. The dome is supported by a wide, angular landing gear structure with two large circular pads or wheels visible at the outer ends of the gear struts. Below the center of the dome, three nozzle-like structures are shown emitting plumes downward, suggesting propulsion. The entire apparatus is depicted over a flat, hazy horizon line, suggesting a landscape or planetary surface. The overall appearance is that of a conceptual design for a VTOL (Vertical Take-Off and Landing) craft or a planetary lander, with annotations illustrating principles of stability or force vectors.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A monochrome technical diagram with the Spanish title “ESTABILIDAD CINÍTDA-GLOBAL Y SEMI-GLOBAL,” showing a symmetrical geometric mesh or dome-like structure over a horizon line, with clouds and dashed construction lines.*
